course_description:
This course prepares pre-service teachers to teach Economics in the senior phase of schooling. Pre-service teachers will be provided with opportunity to develop knowledge and understanding of relevant curriculum documents. The course will engage pre-service teachers in a critical analysis of recent disciplinary developments in scholarship and professional practice in the teaching of Economics. They will be provided with opportunities to develop the appropriate knowledge and teaching strategies to effectively deal with literacy and numeracy demands and learning opportunities that exist in Economics. Pre-service teachers will demonstrate the application of knowledge and skills to practice through (i) designing, implementing and evaluating effective learning and teaching sequences and discipline specific assessment tasks that draw on curriculum, assessment, reporting, and ICT knowledge; and (ii) identifying and designing discipline specific opportunities for professional engagement and communication within the school context, teaching networks and the broader community.

This course has an approved course date variation from the standard semester schedule. Please visit the Course Date Variations page for further details.

course_aims:
This course prepares pre-service teachers to teach Economics in the senior phase of schooling by

1.ﾠ Providing an opportunity to develop knowledge and understanding of relevant curriculum documents.

2.ﾠ Engaging in analysis of recent disciplinary developments in scholarship and professional practice in the teaching of Economics.

3.ﾠ Providing opportunities to develop the appropriate knowledge and teaching strategies to effectively deal with literacy and numeracy demands and learning opportunities that exist in Economics.

4.ﾠ Providing opportunities to demonstrate ability to:

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate understanding of relevant content and - Demonstrate understanding of relevant content and analysis of recent disciplinary developments in scholarship and professional practice in the teaching of Economics. APST 2.1

Demonstrate understanding of relevant content and - Demonstrate the ability to apply knowledge of how students learn and knowledge of relevant curriculum documents to the development of appropriate discipline based learning goals and teaching strategies for students of varying abilities. APST 1.2, 1.5, 2.1, 3.1

Demonstrate understanding of relevant content and - Demonstrate the ability to design, implement and evaluate learning and teaching sequences and discipline specific assessment tasks that draw on relevant curriculum documents, a range of effective teaching resources, a range of assessment and reporting strategies, and ICT knowledge that enables teaching strategies that expands learning. APST 2.2, 2.3, 2.6, 3.2, 3.3, 3.4, 5.1
Demonstrate understanding of relevant content and - Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of literacy and numeracy teaching strategies and their application in Economics. APST 2.5

Demonstrate understanding of relevant content and - Demonstrate the ability to respond to feedback, and identify and design discipline specific opportunities for professional learning engagement and communication within the school context, teaching networks and the broader community and understand the rationale for this professional practice in relation to improved student learning. APST 6.3, 6.4, 7.4
Demonstrate understanding of relevant content and - Demonstrate clear, fluent and coherent communication skills in English consistent with personal, professional and academic conventions. APST 2.1

assessment_summary.title: Microteaching Demonstration
assessment_summary.due_date: Microteaching Presentation 26/03/2026 - 2/04/2026; Reflection 2/04/2026 - 16/04/2026; Presentation Times:; In class 16:00 to 18:50; Reflection; : 1 week after presentation

assessment_details.title: Microteaching Demonstration
assessment_details.due_date: Microteaching Presentation 26/03/2026 - 2/04/2026 Reflection 2/04/2026 - 16/04/2026 Presentation Times: In class 16:00 to 18:50 Reflection : 1 week after presentation
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Context Using the Queensland Economics Syllabus you are to present an interactive teaching activity. This presentation will occur during Semester One. We will dedicate time in our tutorials to microteaching. Each student will present a microteaching segment of 15 minutes that will be graded. The Task You are to select from the lesson topics provided one week in advance from the Economics Syllabus and complete the following: Prior to class Prepare a 60-minute lesson plan to teach an agreed economic concept Prepare to teach a 10-minute interactive active learning component within your lesson plan as your microteaching segment Prepare any resources you are going to use during your 10 minute presentation Note : You need to ensure that if you are using any audio-visual clips, they do not exceed more than 2 minutes of your 10 minute allocated timeslot During class Hand in a hard copy of your lesson plan and a copy of the resources you will use for your microteaching segment. Outline where your activity fits into your lesson using your lesson plan in five minutes. You should refer to past knowledge the students have and other activities that will occur in the lesson in this outline. Conduct your 10 minute interactive learning activity. Due to be drop boxed on Blackboard within one week of your presentation Complete a personal reflection on both your lesson and lesson plan outlining: What went well? What you could improve for next time? What professional learning you need to complete to improve student learning? A rationale outlining the importance of continued professional learning. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Day of presentation: Hand in a hard copy of your lesson plan and a copy of the resources you will use for your microteaching segment.
1 week after presentation: Dropbox reflection on Blackboard
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Late submission
A penalty of 1 grade for each 24 hour period from time submission is due will apply for up to 7 days. After 7 days you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.late_submission: A penalty of 1 grade for each 24 hour period from time submission is due will apply for up to 7 days. After 7 days you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Professional engagement and communication task
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Context After completing your micro teaching task in Semester One and throughout your professional experience placement, you will have reflected on your own personal professional goals in Economics Curriculum Studies to improve your teaching and student outcomes. Throughout Semester Two, we will dedicate time in our tutorials to sharing strategies to address professional development needs in a community context. During this time, each student will facilitate a session of 15 minutes that will be graded. The Task You are to individually select a personal professional goal based on feedback you have received to date: Prior to class Conduct research into how students learn and the implications for teaching which will help you achieve growth towards your personal professional goal. Prepare and submit to TurnItIn a 1500-word report that outlines: Your personal professional goal and an overview of why continued professional learning is essential for teachers including: Background explaining why you identified this goal and how it is linked to feedback you have received from supervisors and teachers; Academic research on strategies to use; Classroom interventions and practical tips; Expected impact on teaching and learning; An overview of how you plan to work with professional associations and community representatives to build your professional knowledge and contribute to enhance teaching practices. Upload an abstract of your presentation to the discussion board on Blackboard (250 words). During class Facilitate a 15-minute session to share what you have learned with your class. After class Respond to at least two students’ submissions suggesting an alternative approach for using these strategies in your own teaching to improve student outcomes. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submit your 1,500 word report to Turnitin on Blackboard and your abstract on the Discussion Board
Facilitate your 15 minute session in class
Respond to at least two other students on the Discussion Board
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Late submission
A penalty of 1 grade for each 24 hour period from time submission is due will apply for up to 7 days. After 7 days you will receive a mark of 0.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension .
assessment_details.late_submission: A penalty of 1 grade for each 24 hour period from time submission is due will apply for up to 7 days. After 7 days you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Learning & teaching sequence & assessment task
assessment_details.task_description: Context To ensure that the learning intents of the syllabus are delivered effectively, teachers need to plan their learning sequences and ensure that assessment tasks meet the requirements of the instrument specific marking guides. The Task You are to individually create a 20-25 lesson unit of work based on the 2025 Queensland Economics Syllabus. You are not permitted to use Unit 1 for this assignment. Your submission needs to include the following: 1. Provide a unit overview in a detailed grid format (use detailed grid template on Blackboard) which outlines: Learning goal/intent Key content/vocab/terminology Key teaching, reporting and learning strategies Demonstrate planning and sequencing content and tasks to become increasingly challenging Incorporate spacing and retrieval practice (CC 2.1 – assessed) Show skill in explicit teaching, modelling and scaffolding practices that support how a student’s brain learns (CC 2.2 – assessed) Literacy and numeracy activities Marzano cognition Resources required Homework Assessment strategies (e.g. informal, formal, diagnostic, formative, summative) 2. Describe and justify the teaching strategies/approaches to be used (with academic references). In this section you are to refer to relevant learning theory. 3. Prepare detailed lesson plans for three key lessons within your unit plan and provide all resources digitally (if you are using physical resources, take a photo and include it with your submission). 4. You need to ensure that at least one of these three lessons demonstrates the implementation of ICT teaching strategies. Justify how these strategies expand curriculum learning opportunities for students. 5. Using one lesson, explain how you have set learning goals that provide achievable challenges for students of varying abilities and characteristics. 6 Explain how in your unit (and detailed lesson plans) you have incorporated strategies to differentiate teaching to meet the specific learning needs of students across the full range of abilities. In addition, demonstrate knowledge of and skill in planning and sequencing content and tasks so that they become increasingly challenging and incorporate spacing and retrieval practice, and demonstrate knowledge of and skill in explicit teaching, modelling and scaffolding practices that support how a student’s brain learns. 7. Explain how in your unit (and detailed lesson plans) you have incorporated strategies to differentiate teaching to meet the specific learning needs of students across the full range of abilities. 8. Explain how literacy and numeracy strategies will be incorporated and developed within your unit. You need to provide specific examples of activities or strategies used. 9. Provide an assessment task and criteria for the unit that reflects the requirements of the curriculum document. Demonstrate your understanding of assessment strategies by describing how you will use a range of approaches throughout the unit (e.g. informal, formal, diagnostic, formative, summative) to assess student learning. Word Count: 1500-2000 which does not include: Item 1 - Unit plan grid overview Item 3 - 3 x lesson plans with associated resources Item 8 - Assessment Task Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Dropbox Assessment Task 3 in one file to Turnitin on Blackboard
Ensure that URLs links can be opened
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Late submission
A penalty of 1 grade for each 24 hour period from time submission is due will apply for up to 7 days. After 7 days you will receive a mark of 0.

learning_activities.topic: Spare Week

Session 1 - Introduction to teaching Economics Workshop 1: Introduction to Teaching Economics. Introducing the QCAA Economics syllabus document (Years 11-12) focusing on the key elements of curriculum, assessment and reporting. We will also explore the QCAA Economics Subject Report. Activities include unpacking perceptions of teaching and learning, distinguishing the skills taught in Economics compared to other subjects and understanding how students learn. Students will also be introduced to professional engagement as an Economics teacher. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L05

Session 2 - Microteaching and Lesson Planning Workshop 2: Microteaching and Lesson Planning. Students will teach a topic of their choice in a 10 minute segment in a supportive environment. This microteaching demonstration will be focused on active learning where the teacher is "facilitating" and the students are "doing". Individual feedback will be provided to each student. Activities include engaging in a class discussion to identify effective strategies that increase engagement and student learning, exploring articles on active learning and introducing a lesson planning template. Students will also experience teaching and learning with ICT. Learning outcomes: L06

Session 3 - Microteaching and Unit Planning Workshop 3: Microteaching and Unit Planning . Students will present a practice run of their micro-teaching including a full lesson plan, identification of their 10 minute active learning segment & reflection. Students will receive feedback from both the Course Coordinator and their peers. Students will start to understand how the syllabus links to unit plans, lesson plans and active learning activities. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L06

Session 4 - Microteaching and Clear Instructions Workshop 4: Microteaching and Clear Instructions. Students will present a practice run of their micro-teaching including a full lesson plan, identification of their 10 minute active learning segment & reflection. Students will receive feedback from both the Course Coordinator and their peers. Students will work in groups to develop clear instructions for scenarios. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L06

Session 5 - Assessment Task 1 - Microteaching Workshop 5: Assessment Task 1 – Microteaching Demonstration. Students to complete their Microteaching Demonstration and receive feedback from their peers . Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05, L06

Session 6 - Assessment Task 1 - Continued Workshop 6: Assessment Task 1 – Microteaching Demonstration. Students to complete their Microteaching Demonstration and receive feedback from their peers . Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05, L06
Mid Semester Break Continue to work on Assessment Task 3

Session 7 - Behaviour Management Workshop 7: Behaviour Management. Students will engage in individual and collaborative scenario activities to explore different approaches to behaviour management. Activities include engaging in a class discussion to identify effective strategies that reduce behaviour management incidents, exploring articles on behaviour management and introducing different frameworks. Learning outcomes: L02, L06

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 8 To Week 12 (20 Apr - 24 May)
learning_activities.topic: Placement 1 EDUC4615 6 Week Block Placement

Session 8: Unit Planning, lesson sequencing, and explicit teaching and scaffolding Workshop 8: Unit planning, lesson sequencing, and explicit teaching and scaffolding . Workshop 8 is split into five mini-lectures, with each followed by tutor-led activities.

Session 9 - Cognitive Verbs of Analyse and Evaluate Workshop 9: Cognitive Verbs of Analyse and Evaluate. Students will complete activities individually and collaboratively to distinguish between the cognitive verbs of analyse and evaluate. Activities including unpacking the syllabus descriptors for each cognition, exploring different teaching strategies and designing activities for the classroom. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L06

Session 10 - Literacy and Numeracy Workshop 10: Literacy and Numeracy. Students will complete activities individually and collaboratively to understand how literacy and numeracy activities can be incorporated in the economics classroom. Activities including unpacking the syllabus requirements, exploring different teaching strategies and designing activities for the classroom. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
Session 11 - Assessment Task 2 Workshop 11: Assessment Task 2. Students will present Assessment Task 2. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05, L06
Session 12 - Assessment Task 2 - Continued Workshop 11: Assessment Task 2. Students will present Assessment Task 2. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05, L06

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 17 To Week 21 (17 Aug - 20 Sep)
learning_activities.topic: Placement 2 5 Week block placement plus spare week
Mid Semester Break Continue to work on Assessment Task 3

Session 13 - Assessment Design & Development - Part 1 Workshop 13: Assessment Design & Development – Part 1. Students will engage with a ssessment design and development including diagnostic, formative and summative approaches to assess student learning and check for understanding. Activities will focus on syllabus requirements and QCAA exemplars. Learning outcomes: L03, L06

Session 14 - Assessment Design & Development - Part 2 Workshop 14: Assessment Design & Development – Part 2. Students will continue work from last week. Students will complete activities individually and collaboratively to write questions that address the cognitions of Comprehending, Analysing and Evaluating. Students will begin to design their own assessment task for their Unit Plan and receive peer feedback. Learning outcomes: L03, L06

Session 15 - Teaching strategies using ICT, creating inclusive classrooms & Professional Associations Workshop 15: Teaching strategies using ICT, creating inclusive classrooms & Professional Associations. Students will explore strategies for using ICT in the economics classroom and creating an inclusive and supportive environment to cater for all students in an Economics classroom (principles of UDL). Students will explore professional learning opportunities and professional engagement as an Economics teacher. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05, L06
Session 16 - Conclusion of the course Workshop 16: Conclusion of the course. Setting you up for your first lesson in your first Economics class. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L06
